description_long: This sculpture depicts a robed human figure, possibly female, standing on a rectangular base. The figure is draped in flowing garments with detailed folds and appears to be holding an object, possibly a skull, in its hands, suggesting a contemplative or mournful pose. The head is slightly bowed, and the facial features are delicately rendered, with a serene or somber expression. At the figure's feet, there is a smaller sculpted head or mask, adding to the narrative complexity of the piece. The surface texture is rough and matte, with visible chisel marks and a weathered appearance, indicating it may be made of terracotta or a similar material. The overall form is solid and monolithic, with no visible openings or interactive components.
description: A robed human figure stands on a rectangular base, holding a small object and featuring detailed drapery and a secondary head at its feet. The sculpture has a rough, matte surface and a contemplative, somber presence.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full robed figure, head bowed, holding an object in its hands, with a smaller head at the base.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the depth of the drapery and the forward-leaning posture, partially obscuring the face and the object held.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the solid, unadorned back of the sculpture, with the drapery forming a continuous mass and no visible details.
description_view_3: This angled rear-side view shows the layered folds of the robe and the thickness of the sculpture, with the head and hands mostly hidden.
attribute_subject_matter: robed human figure holding a skull with a secondary head at its feet
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
